methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Dominion Dynamics is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Eliot Pence that has raised $18.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI—operating in the same sector and at equivalent or adjacent development stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $18.8M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $47M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median across this set is $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $40M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $136M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage progression assumptions and peer heterogeneity—particularly the dispersion among Series A comparables, which span $150M to $500M. Stage advancement multiples and the relevance of more mature peer valuations to an early-stage company represent the primary sources of valuation variance that warrant further scrutiny.
